reasoning: No blocking code issues found. I reviewed the spec, CLAUDE.MD, local project skills, learnings, loop evidence, git diff, unstaged/untracked Plan 4 files, and all new fixtures. The Secret scaffold satisfies FR6 functional requirements, contributes no nix files, uses correct resolver-compatible section headers, documents the current Infisical subprocess form as primary usage, and has the required CLAUDE/skill/docs files. test.cyan.yaml has exactly 9 cases with the required answer_state keys and validators. Direct fixture checks passed for AC7, AC8, AC9, AC10, no leftover template vars, no flake.nix, shell/YAML parsing, and no lone-colon nix headers. Local SDK typings confirm the Cyan SDK API usage. Existing evidence reports 9/9 CyanPrint tests, idempotency, and snapshot stability. My live cyanprint repro failed before test execution during registry dependency resolution, and tsc repro OOMed even with an 8GB heap; these appear to be local tooling/registry/resource limitations rather than Plan 4 implementation defects, especially because Plan 4 did not change TypeScript code. The secrets.sh use of 'infisical run --env=dev -- true' differs from the illustrative bare snippet but satisfies the functional check and is consistent with the documented safer subprocess form, so I do not treat it as a blocker.
